meeting.title: AI Maven Course and Zo Build Planning

reasoning.B04: Multiple unresolved questions: (1) Will people pay for the career coaching hotline? David explicitly says 'I don't know that people are going to pay for that.' (2) What's fair comp for V's tech/Zo access? Left open. (3) How much data does the AI CRM networking tool need to be useful? David raises this directly. (4) Sponsorship model details (Brex, Manifest) are sketched but unspecified. (5) AI Maven course — format, syllabus, cohort timing all deferred.
reasoning.B06: Heavy business strategy discussion: V pitches 'founder maxing' — exclusive weekly build sessions for funded founders at premium pricing. Sponsorship model via Shivam's connections (Manifest, Brex). V's positioning claim: 'I don't think there is another founder that is as smart at me AI that is not just using that towards their company.' Career coaching hotline and CRM networking tool both discussed as potential products. Multiple revenue models floated.
reasoning.B10: Clear relationship evolution: V and David moving from informal check-ins toward structured paid collaboration ($25/3hrs as starter). V explicitly says David is in the trust tier ('you're like one of the tier of folks I would actually trust to connect to my Zo'). Discussion of ongoing co-building sessions, shared tooling, and potential joint products (career coaching hotline combining both their expertise). Partnership is deepening with concrete next steps.
reasoning.B13: Multiple next steps surface: (1) Set regular co-building time at $25/first 3 hours. (2) V to set up Zo-to-Zo connection so David can leverage V's architectural knowledge. (3) Treat first collaborative round as a design cycle, not monetization. (4) V to chat with Jason Rosen re: security review of repo. (5) David to show V what IDO has been building (CRM/networking). (6) Decide which project to tackle first (course vs hotline vs CRM vs personal builds).
reasoning.B25: Specific deliverables discussed: (1) Zo-to-Zo bridge — V gives David temporary access to V's Zo for collaborative building. (2) Career coaching hotline prototype ('Ask David'/'Ask career coach'). (3) CRM-style networking tool. (4) AI Maven course syllabus + cohort structure + lightning lessons. (5) 'Founder maxing' weekly session format and pricing. Each has an implied owner (V leads tech, David co-designs content/use cases).
reasoning.B28: Significant market intelligence: (1) Founders are worried about being left behind on AI — V hears this from 'every founder including dinner last night.' (2) Founders questioning opportunity cost of current company vs learning AI. (3) CRM/networking AI tools trending heavily on X — David says 'I'm hearing and seeing a bunch of stuff about this on X all the time.' (4) Multiple builders converging on same space (IDO, Logan, others). (5) V being treated as engineer-level by CTOs signals credibility shift.
reasoning.B32: Several novel ideas worth capturing: (1) Zo-to-Zo architecture with Zo Pewter as security firewall/concierge — double firewall where attacker must break Zo Pewter AND wrangle it into breaking main Zo. (2) 'Founder maxing' concept — exclusive, tactical, weekly build sessions where funded founders walk out with one concrete productivity tool each week. (3) Sponsorship-funded founder community (Brex, Manifest model). (4) Career coaching hotline at ~$0.50/min as low-cost experiment. (5) Collaborative building model where V's Zo architects and David's Zo executes.
